scope.summary:
Goal: Add LSP `textDocument/signatureHelp` (parameter hints) to the native code editor, with a graceful fallback to the Handshake backend code-nav symbol signature when no language server is attached. This is a PURE ADDITION layered on top of MT-008's LSP JSON-RPC request/response plumbing and MT-010's editor wiring — it introduces a new request method on the existing `LspClient`, a new floating parameter-hint popup widget, and a new fallback path through the existing `CodeNavClient`. It does not modify the text buffer, the completion popup, the hover tooltip, or any backend code.

Behavior to implement (VS Code parity): while the user is typing a function call, signature help is requested automatically when an open-paren `(` or a comma `,` trigger character is typed, and can also be requested manually via Ctrl+Shift+Space. The request sends `textDocument/signatureHelp` at the current cursor position. The response (`SignatureHelp { signatures: Vec<SignatureInformation>, active_signature: u32, active_parameter: u32 }`) is rendered as a small floating parameter-hint popup anchored just above the cursor line. The popup shows the active signature's `label` (e.g. `fn add(a: i32, b: i32) -> i32`) with the currently-active parameter span rendered bold / emphasized (color + bold weight, derived from the signature's `parameters[active_parameter].label`, which may be either a string slice or a `[start,end]` offset pair into the signature label per the LSP 3.17 spec — handle both). The popup is dismissed when the user types a close-paren `)`, presses Escape, or moves the cursor outside the active call's argument list. If a server returns multiple `signatures` (overloads), show the active one and a small `1/N` indicator; Up/Down arrow cycles overloads (active_signature index).

Fallback path: when `lsp_client` is None or returns an empty/None `SignatureHelp`, derive a single synthetic signature from the Handshake backend code-nav symbol under the cursor. Resolve the symbol via the existing `CodeNavClient` (from MT-008): look up the call-target identifier with `lookup_symbols`, take the best match, then `get_symbol(entity_id)` and build the signature label from `CodeSymbolResponse.display_name` plus its `params` (parameter names/types as returned by the backend). The fallback `active_parameter` is computed locally by counting the number of top-level commas between the open-paren of the current call and the cursor (a small paren-depth scanner over the buffer slice). If the backend is unreachable or no symbol matches, render nothing and never panic.

Files to CREATE:
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/signature_help.rs` — the core of this MT. Defines:
    * `SignatureHelpState { signatures: Vec<SignatureInfo>, active_signature: usize, active_parameter: usize, anchor_byte: usize, source: SignatureSource }` where `SignatureSource` is an enum `Lsp | CodeNavFallback`.
    * `SignatureInfo { label: String, parameters: Vec<ParamSpan>, documentation: Option<String> }` and `ParamSpan { label: String, range_in_label: Option<Range<usize>> }` (range_in_label resolved from the LSP `[start,end]` offset form when present, otherwise computed by substring search of `label` in the signature label).
    * `fn active_parameter_from_commas(buffer_slice: &str, open_paren_byte: usize, cursor_byte: usize) -> usize` — top-level-comma counter using a paren/bracket/brace depth scanner that skips commas inside nested calls, string literals, and char literals.
    * `fn render_signature_popup(ui: &mut egui::Ui, state: &SignatureHelpState, cursor_screen_pos: egui::Pos2)` — draws the floating popup (see step-by-step) and registers AccessKit metadata.
    * `fn signature_label_runs(sig: &SignatureInfo, active_param: usize) -> Vec<(String, bool)>` — splits the signature label into (text, is_active) runs so the active parameter renders bold; the active run is the `ParamSpan.range_in_label` for `parameters[active_param]`.
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/lsp_client.rs` — add ONE new method `signature_help(&self, uri: &str, position: LspPosition) -> Option<SignatureHelp>` that sends a `textDocument/signatureHelp` JSON-RPC request over the existing stdio transport (same pending-request / oneshot-channel routing established in MT-008) and deserializes the `lsp_types::SignatureHelp` response. Reuse the existing request-id allocation and reader task — do NOT add a second transport. Return None gracefully when no process is attached or the server has no signatureHelp capability (check the cached `ServerCapabilities.signature_help_provider` from `initialize`; if absent, skip the LSP call and let the caller fall back).
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/pane.rs` — wire the trigger and the render. This is the editor pane integration module (see Conventions note). Add a `signature_help_state: Option<SignatureHelpState>` field to the editor pane struct. In the per-frame update: (a) on input, detect the `(` and `,` trigger characters and the Ctrl+Shift+Space shortcut, and on each trigger spawn an async signature-help request (LSP first, then code-nav fallback) using the same non-blocking channel pattern MT-008 uses for completion/hover (spawn `tokio::task`, poll a result channel on the next frame — never block the egui thread); (b) on `)` or Escape or cursor leaving the call, clear `signature_help_state`; (c) if `signature_help_state.is_some()`, call `signature_help::render_signature_popup(...)` after the text is laid out so the popup floats above the cursor line.

Files to EDIT (module wiring):
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/mod.rs` — add `pub mod signature_help;` so the new module is part of the code_editor module tree (mirror how MT-008 added `lsp_client` / `code_nav`).

Conventions / path reconciliation (READ THIS): MT-008 (the dependency that owns the LSP plumbing) places the editor pane widget at one of two filenames inside `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/` (e.g. `pane.rs` or `panel.rs`), while later E1/E5 MTs reference the pane integration module under that same directory. Both candidate filenames are listed in this contract's allowed_paths so the coder may edit whichever exists in the repo at execution time; the binding rule is: edit the SINGLE existing editor pane/panel module that already owns the cursor + LSP wiring from MT-008/MT-010, and do not create a duplicate. If neither file exists yet, the dependency (MT-008/MT-010) is not merged and this MT is blocked — emit a typed blocker rather than scaffolding a new panel. The native crate layout is FLAT under `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/`; there is no `panes/`, `panels/`, `shared/`, or `interop/` directory at `src/frontend/` — the editor and all its submodules live under `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/`.

Exact crates / APIs:
- `lsp-types = "0.95"` (already added in MT-008) — use `lsp_types::SignatureHelp`, `SignatureInformation`, `ParameterInformation`, `ParameterLabel::{Simple, LabelOffsets}`, `SignatureHelpParams`, `TextDocumentPositionParams`. Map `lsp_types` shapes into the local `SignatureHelpState` so the renderer is transport-agnostic (LSP and code-nav fallback both produce `SignatureHelpState`).
- `egui` — render the popup with `egui::Area::new(egui::Id::new("code_editor_signature_help")).order(egui::Order::Tooltip).fixed_pos(anchor_pos).show(ctx, |ui| { egui::Frame::popup(ui.style()).show(ui, ...) })`. Build the label as a sequence of `egui::RichText` runs from `signature_label_runs`, the active run with `.strong()` and an emphasis color from `ui.visuals()`. Anchor the popup ABOVE the cursor line (offset cursor_screen_pos.y by minus the popup height) so it does not cover the text being typed; if there is no room above, fall back to below.
- `accesskit` — register the popup root node with `author_id = "code_editor_signature_help"` and `Role::Tooltip` (matches the hover-tooltip convention from MT-008, and reuse the existing AccessKit registration helpers in `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/`). Include the full active-signature label as the node's name/value so a no-context swarm agent can read the hint without pixels.
- `tokio` — reuse the existing async runtime and the MT-008 non-blocking result-channel pattern; do NOT introduce a new runtime or block the frame.

Handshake backend API bound (fallback only): `GET /knowledge/code/symbols/{entity_id}` (the existing `CodeNavClient::get_symbol` from MT-008) supplies `display_name` + `params` for the synthetic fallback signature, and `GET /knowledge/code/symbols?workspace_id=...&prefix=...` (`CodeNavClient::lookup_symbols`) resolves the call-target identifier to an entity_id. No NEW backend route is introduced or required. PostgreSQL/EventLedger backend only (no SQLite). LSP `textDocument/signatureHelp` is stdio JSON-RPC, NOT an HTTP route.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. Add `pub mod signature_help;` to `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/mod.rs`.
2. In `lsp_client.rs`, cache `signature_help_provider` (and its `trigger_characters` / `retrigger_characters`) from the `initialize` response `ServerCapabilities`; expose a `supports_signature_help() -> bool` and the trigger char set so the pane uses server-declared triggers when available, defaulting to `(` and `,` otherwise.
3. Add `LspClient::signature_help` reusing the existing JSON-RPC send/await: allocate a request id, send `textDocument/signatureHelp` with `SignatureHelpParams`, await the oneshot, deserialize to `lsp_types::SignatureHelp`, convert to local `SignatureHelpState` (resolve `ParameterLabel::LabelOffsets([u32;2])` into `range_in_label`, and `ParameterLabel::Simple(String)` into a substring search of the signature label).
4. In the editor pane, add the trigger detection in the input phase: a typed `(` or `,` (only when not inside a string/char literal at the cursor — reuse the depth scanner), or Ctrl+Shift+Space. On trigger, spawn the request task; on result, set `signature_help_state`.
5. Compute `active_parameter` from the LSP response when present; for the fallback path, compute it locally via `active_parameter_from_commas` over the buffer slice from the call's open-paren to the cursor.
6. Fallback resolution: identify the call-target identifier token to the left of the open-paren; `lookup_symbols(prefix)` -> best match -> `get_symbol(entity_id)` -> build `SignatureInfo` from `display_name` + `params`. Cache the resolved signature per (identifier, open_paren_byte) for the lifetime of the popup so commas do not re-hit the backend.
7. Render with `render_signature_popup`: floating Area, popup frame, label runs with the active parameter bold, optional `1/N` overload indicator, optional first line of documentation. Anchor above the cursor line.
8. Dismissal: clear state on `)`, Escape, cursor exiting the argument list (paren depth returns to the call's base depth), or when the editor loses focus.
9. AccessKit: emit the `code_editor_signature_help` Tooltip node with the active signature label text whenever the popup is visible.
10. Tests: see expected_tests / proof_targets — one mock-LSP request test (active param parsing), one graceful fallback test (no LSP -> code-nav signature, no panic), one kittest screenshot proving the active parameter is emphasized, one kittest AccessKit test proving the Tooltip node exists.

Dependencies / unblock conditions: MT-008 (LspClient stdio JSON-RPC transport + CodeNavClient backend code-nav bindings) and MT-010 (editor pane wiring that this hooks into) must be merged. arboard/clipboard are NOT involved. If the LSP transport or the editor pane wiring is absent, emit a typed blocker rather than re-implementing them.
Risk if missed: No parameter hints while typing function calls — a core IntelliSense parity gap versus the React Monaco surface, which provided signature help via Monaco's built-in provider. This blocks MT-044 (the E1 parity proof / aggregation that asserts the full IntelliSense feature set).
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: `LspClient::signature_help` sends a `textDocument/signatureHelp` JSON-RPC request over the EXISTING MT-008 stdio transport (no second transport added) and deserializes the response into the local `SignatureHelpState` — proven by `cargo test -p handshake-native signature_help_request` against a mock LSP that returns one `SignatureInformation`; the parsed `active_parameter` index matches the mock.

AC-002: Trigger characters `(` and `,` and the manual shortcut Ctrl+Shift+Space all initiate a signature-help request; `)` and Escape dismiss the popup; cursor leaving the argument list dismisses the popup — proven by a kittest interaction test.

AC-003: Graceful fallback — with no language server attached, `signature_help` returns None and the pane derives a synthetic signature from the backend code-nav symbol (`display_name` + `params`) via the existing `CodeNavClient`; if the backend is also unreachable, nothing renders and no panic occurs — proven by `cargo test -p handshake-native signature_help_graceful`.

AC-004: The popup renders the active parameter emphasized (bold + emphasis color) distinctly from the other parameters; both LSP `ParameterLabel::LabelOffsets` and `ParameterLabel::Simple` forms resolve correctly to the active run — proven by the `signature_help_popup` egui_kittest screenshot (the active-parameter run is visibly emphasized vs inactive runs).

AC-005: AccessKit tree contains a node with `author_id='code_editor_signature_help'` and `role=Tooltip` whose text content includes the active signature label — proven by an egui_kittest AccessKit dump.

AC-006: This MT is a pure addition — the text buffer, completion popup, hover tooltip, and all `src/backend/**` code are unchanged; the only edit to `lsp_client.rs` is the additive `signature_help` method plus capability caching — verified by diff review of allowed_paths only.
AC-007: `active_parameter_from_commas` correctly ignores commas nested inside inner calls, string literals, and char literals — proven by a unit test over a representative argument string.
AC-008: No `unwrap()`/`expect()` on LSP-response or backend-response parsing paths; malformed or missing fields degrade to None/empty, never panic.
proof_targets: PT-001: `cargo test -p handshake-native signature_help_request -- --nocapture` — mock LSP returns one SignatureInformation; active param index parsed correctly; GREEN, logged.
PT-002: `cargo test -p handshake-native signature_help_graceful -- --nocapture` — no LSP attached -> code-nav fallback returns a signature, no panic; GREEN, logged.

PT-003: egui_kittest screenshot test `signature_help_popup` — popup shows the active parameter emphasized; PNG saved to `test_output/MT-047-signature-help-popup.png`, emphasis verified by pixel/run inspection.
PT-004: egui_kittest AccessKit dump test `signature_help_accesskit` — node `author_id='code_editor_signature_help'`, `role=Tooltip` present with the active signature label in its text content.
PT-005: `cargo test -p handshake-native active_parameter_from_commas` — top-level comma counting ignores nested calls and string/char literals; GREEN.
implementation_notes: Reuse the EXACT MT-008 non-blocking pattern: never call the LSP or backend from the egui render loop; spawn `tokio::task` on trigger events and poll a result channel on the next frame.

Cross-WP dependency (WP-011): the AccessKit registration helpers and Tooltip-node conventions live in the WP-011 module `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/` (mod.rs, live.rs, registry.rs, snapshot.rs); reuse them for the `code_editor_signature_help` Tooltip node rather than registering AccessKit nodes ad hoc.

LSP `ParameterInformation.label` is `ParameterLabel`: `Simple(String)` OR `LabelOffsets([u32; 2])` (byte offsets into the signature label). Handle BOTH — prefer `LabelOffsets` when present; otherwise substring-search the simple label within the signature label to compute `range_in_label`.
Respect server-declared `signatureHelpProvider.triggerCharacters` and `retriggerCharacters` from `initialize` when present; default to `(` and `,` when absent.

Anchor the popup ABOVE the cursor line so it does not occlude the text being typed; fall back to below when there is no vertical room. Use `egui::Order::Tooltip` so it floats over the editor without taking keyboard focus.

Retrigger semantics: typing a `,` should UPDATE the active parameter of the already-open popup (re-request or recompute active_parameter), not open a second popup. Key the popup by the call's open-paren byte offset.
Fallback signature caching: cache the resolved `SignatureInfo` per (call-target identifier, open_paren_byte) for the popup lifetime so commas do not re-hit `/knowledge/code/symbols`.
Define `SIGNATURE_HELP_TRIGGER_CHARS = ['(', ',']` and the manual shortcut `Ctrl+Shift+Space` as named constants with comments.

React reference: in `app/src/lib/monaco/code_intelligence.ts`, `markdownForSymbol` builds the symbol signature/markdown shown by Monaco; the native fallback signature label must carry the same `display_name` + params content.

PATH + REAL-SHAPE + EXTERNAL-SCREENSHOT (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-24): the editor pane is code_editor/panel.rs (CodeEditorPanel) - pane.rs does NOT exist (verified); edit panel.rs (the MT-008/010 cursor+LSP wiring) + lsp_client.rs (additive signature_help + capability cache ONLY - no 2nd transport/runtime, AC-006) + signature_help.rs (new) + mod.rs (pub mod). The code-nav FALLBACK reuses the EXISTING MT-008 CodeNavClient in code_editor/code_nav.rs (lookup_symbols + get_symbol) - VERIFY the REAL symbol response shape: MT-039 found the real code-nav symbol is SymbolNavProjection (symbol_key/display_name/symbol_kind/definition/staleness) - it may NOT have a literal 'params' field; build the fallback SignatureInfo label from the REAL fields the backend returns (display_name + whatever param/signature data exists), NOT an assumed 'params' (the verify-over-prose discipline). SCREENSHOT: write to the EXTERNAL Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test/wp-kernel-012-mt-047/ root + assert_no_local_artifact_dir() - the contract's 'test_output/MT-047-...png' is a REPO-LOCAL path that VIOLATES the CX-212E artifact-hygiene rule; git ls-files src/**/*.png must stay empty.

CORRECTNESS GATES (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): active_parameter_from_commas is a DEPTH-AWARE scanner - skip commas inside nested (), [], {}, generic <> angle brackets, AND string/char literals (RISK-1/MC-1, AC-007 - unit-test all these nested cases). The popup is a NON-FOCUS-TAKING egui::Order::Tooltip Area - it must NOT capture keyboard or swallow the next typed char (RISK-3/MC-3 - the MT-008 completion-popup bug class; route keystrokes through the editor first). ParameterLabel BOTH forms: prefer LabelOffsets([u32;2]) when present; for Simple(String) search left-to-right consuming earlier matched ranges so a repeated param name maps to a DISTINCT span (RISK-5/MC-5). NO unwrap()/expect() on LSP or backend parse paths - malformed/missing -> None/empty, never panic (AC-008). Reuse the MT-008 non-blocking tokio task + result-channel pattern (NEVER call LSP/backend from the egui render loop). Retrigger on ',' UPDATES the open popup's active_parameter (keyed by open-paren byte), does NOT open a 2nd popup; cache the fallback signature per (identifier, open_paren_byte) so commas don't re-hit /knowledge/code/symbols (RISK-2/MC-2). AccessKit code_editor_signature_help Tooltip node carries the active signature label text (swarm reads without pixels, MC-006). Provable WITHOUT live PG (mock LSP for the request test; the code-nav fallback via a spy/gated client). If MT-008/010 pane+LSP wiring is absent -> typed blocker (don't scaffold).
ports_from_react: app/src/lib/monaco/code_intelligence.ts — the signature/markdownForSymbol provider: how the symbol signature (display_name + params) is composed for display; port this composition into the code-nav fallback SignatureInfo label, and mirror the LSP signature-help provider behavior Monaco registered for parameter hints.
binds_backend_api: GET /knowledge/code/symbols/{entity_id} — fallback signature source (display_name + params), via the existing CodeNavClient::get_symbol from MT-008; no new route.

GET /knowledge/code/symbols?workspace_id=...&prefix=... — resolves the call-target identifier to an entity_id for the fallback, via the existing CodeNavClient::lookup_symbols from MT-008; no new route.
LSP textDocument/signatureHelp — stdio JSON-RPC over the existing MT-008 LspClient transport, NOT an HTTP route; no backend involvement.

risks: RISK-001: `active_parameter` miscount on the fallback path when commas appear inside nested calls, string literals, char literals, or generic-type angle brackets, highlighting the wrong parameter. Mitigate: a depth-aware scanner (paren/bracket/brace + string/char literal state) is required and unit-tested across nested cases (AC-007).

RISK-002: Re-triggering on every comma re-hits the backend in the fallback path, flooding `/knowledge/code/symbols`. Mitigate: cache the resolved signature per (identifier, open_paren_byte) for the popup lifetime; only re-request on a new call site.

RISK-003: The signature popup steals keyboard focus and swallows the next typed character (same class of bug as the MT-008 completion popup). Mitigate: render via `egui::Order::Tooltip` Area that does NOT take focus; route all keystrokes through the editor first.

RISK-004: A second transport or runtime is accidentally introduced instead of reusing MT-008's stdio JSON-RPC + tokio result-channel pattern, fragmenting the LSP plumbing. Mitigate: the only allowed edit to lsp_client.rs is the additive `signature_help` method plus capability caching (AC-006), enforced by diff review.

RISK-005: LSP `ParameterLabel::Simple` substring search matches the wrong occurrence when a parameter name repeats in the signature label, emphasizing the wrong run. Mitigate: prefer `LabelOffsets` when present; for the Simple form, search left-to-right consuming earlier matched ranges so each parameter maps to a distinct span.
RISK-006: Popup occludes the line being typed or renders off-screen near the editor edges. Mitigate: anchor above the cursor line with a below-line fallback and clamp the Area to the editor viewport.

RISK-007: Path ambiguity between the editor pane filename `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/pane.rs` and `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/panel.rs` leads to a duplicated editor module. Mitigate: edit the single existing pane/panel that owns the MT-008/MT-010 cursor+LSP wiring; if neither exists, emit a typed blocker (dependency not merged) instead of scaffolding.
minimum_controls: MC-001: `active_parameter_from_commas` MUST be unit-tested with nested calls, string literals, and char literals (AC-007 / PT-005).
MC-002: A graceful-degradation test (`signature_help_graceful`) is required and MUST pass with no language server and with the backend unreachable, asserting no panic (AC-003 / PT-002).
MC-003: The signature popup MUST be a non-focus-taking `egui::Order::Tooltip` Area; no keyboard focus capture.
MC-004: The only edit to `lsp_client.rs` is the additive `signature_help` method plus capability caching; no transport/runtime changes (AC-006).
MC-005: Both `ParameterLabel::LabelOffsets` and `ParameterLabel::Simple` forms MUST resolve to the correct active run, verified by the `signature_help_popup` screenshot and the request test.
MC-006: AccessKit Tooltip node `code_editor_signature_help` MUST carry the active signature label text so swarm agents can read the hint without pixels (AC-005 / PT-004).

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + real code_editor/ tree (panel.rs + lsp_client.rs + code_nav.rs) + MT-008 transport + MT-039 SymbolNavProjection shape + CX-212E artifact rule + MT-008 popup-focus bug
corrections: panel.rs not pane.rs; additive signature_help on the MT-008 transport (no 2nd transport/runtime); fallback reuses code_editor/code_nav.rs
verify the REAL code-nav symbol shape (SymbolNavProjection, not assumed 'params') for the fallback label

external screenshot root (not repo-local test_output/); depth-aware comma scanner (skip nested+strings+generics); non-focus-steal Tooltip; both ParameterLabel forms; no unwrap; provable without live PG

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_e2cf0004 (impl + 3-lens panel + harden)
implementation_result.proofs: AC-001..008 + PT-001..005 PASS; 16 unit + 6 integration + 1153 lib + regression (keymap 12, lsp_client, completion/hover, accesskit, E7) green; clippy clean; signature-help popup (fn add(a: i32, b: i32) with active param 'b' BOLD + doc line, anchored above the call) visually verified - VS Code parameter-hint parity; screenshot EXTERNAL (git ls-files src/**/*.png empty).
implementation_result.steers_held: panel.rs (pane.rs absent); LSP signature_help ADDITIVE on the MT-008 transport (generalized to Box<dyn AsyncWrite>/Option<Child> so the test drives the REAL request path in-memory - NO 2nd transport/runtime, AC-006); fallback from_code_nav uses the REAL SymbolNavProjection.display_name (MT-039 shape, no assumed 'params'); depth-aware comma scanner (skips nested ()[]{}<>/string/char; 'a < b' not a generic); non-focus-steal Tooltip + MT-010 single-dispatch keymap preserved; both ParameterLabel forms (LabelOffsets+Simple, repeated-name distinct spans); no unwrap on parse (clamps + char-boundary snap); AccessKit code_editor_signature_help Tooltip carries the label (swarm-readable).
implementation_result.external_screenshot_held: Screenshot to the EXTERNAL wp-kernel-012-mt-047 root (NOT the contract's repo-local test_output/ - CX-212E hygiene held).
implementation_result.waiver_granted: code_editor/signature_help.rs (new) + lsp_client.rs (additive) + mod.rs + panel.rs + test in allowed_paths; code_nav.rs unchanged (read-only fallback reuse).
